sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: |【即時新聞】TrueShares Structured Outcome (October) ETF宣布年度分配，股息每單位0.93美元
feed.summary: |The TrueShares Structured Outcome (October) ETF (OCTZ) has announced an annual distribution of $0.93 per unit, payable on December 26, 2025, with a record and ex-dividend date of December 24, 2025. The ETF's share price recently closed at $42.9248, experiencing a 3.55% decrease, and saw a significant 94.34% drop in trading volume, with only 500 units traded. This decrease in volume may indicate reduced market interest in the structured outcome ETF.

feed.title: PepsiCo (PEP) is in the Crosshairs of Weight Loss Drugs
feed.summary: Fundsmith Equity Fund, in its Q4 2025 investor letter, identified PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) as being vulnerable to the growing impact of weight loss drugs due to their potential to reduce appetite. The fund also noted headwinds for alcoholic beverage companies from Generation Z's drinking habits. Despite acknowledging the risks, Insider Monkey suggests that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside potential.

feed.title: Here’s Why Fortinet (FTNT) Slid in 2025
feed.summary: Fundsmith Equity Fund's 2025 investor letter highlighted Fortinet (FTNT) as a stock that experienced a decline. The fund attributed its overall underperformance in 2025 to index concentration, growth in index funds, and dollar weakness. Fortinet's shares slid due to a fall after "Covid related highs" and disappointment over potential revenue from a renewal cycle, despite the company's strong revenue growth in Q3 2025.

feed.title: TJX Companies Issues Phone Charger Recall Over Fire Risk
feed.summary: TJX Companies announced a recall of Isla Rae Magnetic Wireless Chargers sold at Marshalls and T.J. Maxx due to a fire and burn risk. The recall, involving approximately 13,200 units in the U.S. and 7,000 in Canada, instructs customers to stop using the chargers immediately, register for a refund online, and dispose of the batteries safely according to local regulations. UBS analyst Jay Sole maintained a Buy rating on TJX, raising the price target from $181 to $193.

feed.title: Latham & Watkins Advises Realty Income Corporation on US$862.5 Million Convertible Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Latham & Watkins LLP advised Realty Income Corporation on the closing of its private offering of US$862.5 million aggregate principal amount of 3.5% convertible senior notes due 2029. This offering includes the full exercise of a US$112.5 million option granted to initial purchasers. The legal team from Latham & Watkins was led by partners Darren Guttenberg and Lewis Kneib, with additional advice on convertible notes and tax matters.

feed.title: RIA moves: Raymond James, Ameriprise pick up teams in Texas, Colorado
feed.summary: Raymond James and Ameriprise Financial have each recruited experienced advisory teams, intensifying competition for established practices. Raymond James welcomed Dignum Financial Partners, managing $420 million, from Commonwealth to its independent channel in Texas. Ameriprise added Loveday Caruso Wealth Management Group, overseeing $120 million, from Prudential Advisors to its independent channel in Colorado.

feed.title: Microsoft Wants to Power Retail Without Competing With It
feed.summary: Microsoft is expanding into agentic commerce with its new Copilot Checkout, allowing users to complete purchases within its AI chatbot while retailers manage fulfillment. The company aims to provide AI tools and cloud infrastructure to retailers, enhancing their operations without competing for customer relationships. Analysts remain bullish on Microsoft (MSFT) stock, seeing it as well-positioned in the AI stack despite recent dips, with a Goldman Sachs price target of $655.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley's new VPs include a 25-year-old Scottish sheep farmer in New York
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley recently promoted Scott Ritchie, a 25-year-old former sheep farmer from Scotland, to Vice President in New York. Ritchie's journey highlights the success of apprenticeships and middle-office roles, demonstrating that direct financial services experience isn't always necessary for career progression in banking. His rapid rise, supported by a "rising star" designation and a former US CFO, showcases how diverse backgrounds and strong work ethic can lead to significant advancement.

feed.title: International Business Machines: How IBM Is Re?Wiring Enterprise AI for the Next Decade
feed.summary: International Business Machines is transforming from a legacy hardware company into an AI-first, hybrid-cloud powerhouse by focusing on enterprise AI with its watsonx platform, Red Hat-powered hybrid cloud, and deep industry-specific solutions. IBM's strategy prioritizes governance, data locality, and hybrid deployment, aiming to provide a controllable and auditable AI foundation for heavily regulated industries. This approach positions IBM as a crucial player in the enterprise AI landscape, especially for organizations that need to integrate new AI capabilities with decades of existing systems and strict regulatory requirements.

feed.title: HEINZ Unveils the First Fry Box with a Built-In Condiment Compartment in Eleven Countries Across the Globe
feed.summary: HEINZ has introduced "The HEINZ Dipper," a new fry box with a built-in ketchup compartment designed for on-the-go dipping. This innovation aims to solve the common problem of mess and inconvenience when eating fries with ketchup outside of the home. The Dipper is launching in eleven countries, including the U.S., Canada, and Germany, as part of a global activation.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines Selects GE Aerospace GEnx Engines to Power New 787-10s
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines has chosen GE Aerospace's GEnx engines to power its new fleet of 30 Boeing 787-10 aircraft, with options for 30 more. The agreement also includes spare engines and long-term service support. This partnership, which builds on a long-standing relationship between the two companies, aims to enhance Delta's international growth plans through the GEnx engine's reliability and efficiency.

feed.title: CDW Corp Stock: Quiet Rally, Firm Fundamentals and a Market That Is Finally Paying Attention
feed.summary: CDW Corp's stock has been experiencing a quiet rally, driven by strong institutional conviction, resilient margins, and a constructive chart pattern. Despite low headline activity, the IT services company is being recognized by Wall Street with numerous "Buy" ratings, as it benefits from long-term IT modernization trends in cloud, security, and workplace infrastructure. The company's focus on end-to-end IT solutions, diversified customer base, and strong free cash flow generation position it as a reliable compounder for investors seeking quality exposure to enterprise tech spending.
